creator: martinp@tildeslash.com
text:
Hello,

i have reviewed the patch and - it doesn't address the problem. If some =
daemon/service is starting slowly on background, the rc script can still =
start monit before the slow service finishes even with the proposed =
patch.

The clean solution is to let monit start the service which eliminates =
the race condition (this is common approach for other service management =
software too.)

If the user wants to keep service startup by rc and just suppress the =
monitoring for some well-known "settle" period , he can use the =
"startdelay" option in the monit configuration file like this:

set daemon 5 with startdelay 60


=3D> i do not agree with the patch


Best regards,
Martin


On Oct 13, 2011, at 2:34 AM, Edwin Groothuis wrote:

> Maintainer of sysutils/monit,
